title: Taking the Bus
context: Asking for directions on public transport

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 请问，去火车站坐几路车？
lines.pinyin: Qǐngwèn, qù huǒchēzhàn zuò jǐ lù chē?
lines.english: Excuse me, which bus goes to the train station?

lines.speaker: B
lines.pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ zuò sān lù chē.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 从这里到火车站要多长时间？
lines.pinyin: Cóng zhèlǐ dào huǒchēzhàn yào duō cháng shíjiān?
lines.english: How long does it take from here to the train station?

lines.speaker: A
lines.pinyin: Jīntiān bǐ zuótiān lěng duō le.
lines.english: Today is much colder than yesterday.

lines.speaker: B
lines.pinyin: Shì a, yào xià yǔ le.

lines.speaker: A
lines.pinyin: Wǒ yǒu liǎng bǎ, gěi nǐ yì bǎ ba.
lines.english: I have two, let me give you one.

lines.speaker: B
lines.pinyin: Wǒ tóu téng, hái yǒudiǎnr fāshāo.
lines.english: I have a headache, and I have a slight fever.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 你是什么时候开始不舒服的？
lines.pinyin: Nǐ shì shénme shíhòu kāishǐ bù shūfu de?
lines.english: When did you start feeling unwell?

lines.speaker: A
lines.pinyin: Nǐ yào duō hē shuǐ, duō xiūxi.
lines.english: You should drink more water and rest more.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: What do you want to do this weekend?

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 我正在想去爬山还是去游泳。
lines.pinyin: Wǒ zhèngzài xiǎng qù pá shān háishi qù yóuyǒng.
lines.english: I'm thinking about whether to go hiking or swimming.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 我觉得爬山比游泳有意思。
lines.pinyin: Wǒ juéde pá shān bǐ yóuyǒng yǒu yìsi.
lines.english: I think hiking is more fun than swimming.

lines.speaker: B
lines.pinyin: Hǎo de, nà wǒmen yìqǐ qù ba!
